blocks.content:
You can configure panels, rows, and tabs to be shown or hidden based on rules. For example, you can set a panel to be hidden if there’s no data returned by a query or a tab to only be shown if a specific variable value is present.



In this guide, you'll learn how to configure a show/hide rule that displays a panel only when the dashboard time range is one hour or less. This helps keep dashboards focused by showing time-sensitive panels only when they're relevant to the selected time range.
Create a new dashboard by clicking **New**, then selecting **New dashboard**.
Add a panel, then click **Configure visualization**.

blocks.reftarget: div[data-testid='input-wrapper'] input[data-testid='data-testid Select a data source']
blocks.content: Open the data source selector, search for `-- Gra`, and select the **-- Grafana --** data source.

Set the dashboard layout to **Auto**. 

Your dashboard needs to be in auto layout to use show/hide rules so that it can dynamically reorganize when panels appear and disappear.
Click the panel header to bring it back into focus. Now the sidebar shows panel configuration options.
Now let's add a rule. Click **Add rule**, then select **Time range less than**.

blocks.reftarget: {grafana:pages.Dashboard.Sidebar.conditionalRendering.timeRange.select} div:has(input[data-testid='data-testid Dashboard Conditional Rendering Time range select-input'])
blocks.content:
Set the time range to **1 hour**.

Now, the dashboard only displays this panel if the dashboard time range is 1 hour or less.

The panel now displays the hidden icon and won't be visible in view mode.

Now let's change the dashboard time range to 1 hour. This will make the panel visible again.

Well done! Now you know:

- The dashboard layout requirements for using show/hide rules.
- How to access find show/hide rules in the dashboard sidebar.
- How to configure a time-based show/hide rule.
